questions.question: Which CSS property defines the distance between the viewer and the z=0 plane for 3D-transformed elements?
questions.explanation: The `perspective` property sets the distance from the user to the z=0 plane, creating the illusion of depth for child elements with 3D transforms. A smaller value produces a more pronounced 3D effect.

questions.question: What does `backface-visibility: hidden` do?
choices: Hides the element when it overlaps another element
Makes the back side of a rotated element invisible
Prevents the element from being flipped
Disables all 3D transforms on the element
questions.explanation: When an element is rotated in 3D so its back face would be visible to the user, `backface-visibility: hidden` makes the element invisible rather than showing a mirrored version of its front face.

questions.question: Which `transform-style` value allows child elements to maintain their own 3D positioning?
questions.explanation: Setting `transform-style: preserve-3d` on a parent element allows its children to be positioned in 3D space rather than being flattened onto the parent's plane.

questions.question: Which function rotates an element around the Y axis in 3D space?
questions.explanation: The `rotateY()` function rotates an element around the vertical (Y) axis, creating a turning or flipping effect in 3D space.

questions.question: The ___ function moves an element along the Z axis, making it appear closer to or farther from the viewer.
questions.explanation: The `translateZ()` function moves an element forward (positive values) or backward (negative values) along the Z axis in 3D space, requiring a perspective to be set on a parent element for the effect to be visible.

questions.question: The ___ function rotates an element around the X axis, creating a tilting effect toward or away from the viewer.
questions.explanation: The `rotateX()` function rotates an element around the horizontal (X) axis, making the top tilt away from or toward the viewer depending on the angle.
